author: Corpus Analyst Rowan-C

dimensionSummaries.dimension.player-fantasy: The player inhabits a self-directed wilderness survivor who reads terrain, weather, materials, creatures, and ruins to choose a personally meaningful route through Hyrule.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.core-loop: The cadence is to sight a landmark, plan an approach, traverse or improvise through local conditions, discover a shrine, quest, resource, or memory, and use that gain to pursue a farther curiosity.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.verbs: Running, climbing, gliding, swimming, riding, cooking, gathering, stealth, melee, archery, shield use, physics manipulation, elemental interaction, and photography overlap across traversal and combat.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.world-state: Activated towers and shrines, completed quests, recovered memories, defeated Divine Beasts, opened travel points, weather, time, fire, electricity, and moved objects all change present options at different persistence scales.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.topology: A continuous three-dimensional landscape uses visible towers, peaks, settlements, roads, geological barriers, and Hyrule Castle as nested landmarks rather than a mandatory corridor graph.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.gating-revisit: The plateau teaches required capabilities, after which most gates are soft constraints of stamina, temperature, equipment, knowledge, danger, or chosen preparation rather than a fixed dungeon sequence.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.puzzle-information: Material properties, environmental reactions, silhouettes, airflow, magnetic highlighting, sound, NPC clues, and shrine geometry let the player form multiple physical hypotheses.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.dungeon-structure: Numerous compact shrines isolate rule experiments, Divine Beasts provide larger reconfigurable challenge spaces, and Hyrule Castle permits several routes through a hostile final landmark.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.exploration-reward: Curiosity yields shrines, Korok seeds, resources, equipment, memories, quests, vistas, travel points, and knowledge that can be useful even when no permanent item is awarded.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.growth-economy: Spirit Orbs, stamina, hearts, inventory slots, armor upgrades, rupees, food, elixirs, arrows, materials, and replaceable weapons form several interacting preparation economies.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.combat-boss: Encounters support direct fighting, stealth, terrain, elemental reactions, remote bombs, physics, avoidance, and preparation; bosses foreground readable attacks and champion-related abilities.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.npc-quest: Settlements, stables, travelers, researchers, and regional leaders offer rumors, services, contextual tasks, and reactions that turn self-directed travel into local social purpose.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.narrative-performance: An amnesiac opening aligns player and protagonist knowledge, while optional memories and regional quests reveal the past in a route-dependent order around a stable rescue objective.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.camera-blocking: A freely controlled third-person camera supports horizon scouting, close combat reading, vertical route inspection, Sheikah Slate aiming, and cinematic reframing during major reveals.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.teaching-feedback: The plateau safely introduces powers and survival constraints, while elemental effects, stamina, noise, temperature, weapon warnings, map marks, and physics responses continuously explain consequences.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.ui-input: Health, stamina, temperature, sound, weather, equipment, runes, map pins, quest records, and inventory surfaces provide layered planning information without prescribing one route.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.recovery-accessibility: Frequent saves, fast travel, retreat, cooking, route changes, avoidance, fairies, and later revival effects allow recovery from danger without removing environmental or resource consequences.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.perspective-implementation: The 3D view makes elevation and distant landmarks primary planning data, but requires robust camera control, climbing surfaces, line of sight, streaming, and fall-state readability.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.hardware-multiplayer: The original is a single-player experience released simultaneously on Switch and Wii U; no cooperative state is required, while controller motion can supplement selected aiming interactions.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.production-runtime: Its principles require streamed open terrain, systemic physics and elements, weather and time, climbable geometry, AI perception, dynamic encounters, layered save state, map discovery, and broad interaction testing.

notApplicableDimensions.dimension.hardware-multiplayer: The primary design is single-player and no core causal rule depends on multiplayer, asymmetric screens, networking, or a platform-exclusive input device; platform editions are handled as version deltas.

observationThemes.context: From a high or open position, a distant tower, shrine, settlement, mountain, or other landmark is visible without being assigned as the current objective.
preconditions: The player has free movement beyond the opening constraints
The landmark is within sight or has been marked
playerInput: Aim the camera or scope and place a pin
Climb, walk, ride, or glide toward the landmark
before: The destination is only a visual hypothesis
The terrain between player and landmark is not yet understood
action: Commit to a self-selected route and adapt to elevation and local obstacles
after: The player reaches a new vantage, route branch, or destination
Additional landmarks and local opportunities become visible
feedback: The landmark remains spatially coherent across the journey
Stamina, height, weather, and camera sightlines expose route viability
consequences: The next goal can emerge from the new view instead of a prescribed sequence
Personal spatial memory grows around landmarks and traversed terrain
observationThemes.factualDescription: The continuous 3D landscape exposes distant landmarks that can be pinned and approached through player-chosen combinations of walking, climbing, riding, and gliding.
observationThemes.interpretation: When a destination is visible before its route is known, traversal itself becomes a self-authored problem and each arrival can generate the next curiosity.

observationThemes.context: A flammable object or patch of dry vegetation lies between the player and a local route, resource, or threat.
preconditions: A visibly combustible material is present
The player can introduce or avoid a fire source
before: The local route or tactical situation has not changed
after: The material burns or is removed and heat may create a local updraft
A route, vantage, or encounter plan changes
feedback: Flame, smoke, sound, material disappearance, and airflow make the thermal transition visible
Comparable materials repeat the same response
consequences: The player can incorporate the thermal rule into traversal or combat planning
The local solution remains one instance of a broader consistent material system
observationThemes.factualDescription: Combustible materials can catch fire, visibly burn, and sometimes create heat-driven airflow, changing nearby traversal or encounter conditions.
observationThemes.interpretation: One narrow material rule becomes portable design evidence when its before state, trigger, feedback, and route consequence are independently testable.

observationThemes.context: A shrine is spotted or sensed off the current route and can become a compact optional challenge and future travel point.
preconditions: The shrine has not been completed
The player can reach its entrance through current preparation or an alternate route
playerInput: Enter and solve the bounded challenge
before: The shrine is an unresolved landmark
Its travel point and Spirit Orb are unavailable
after: The shrine records completion and becomes a fast-travel anchor
The player receives an Orb toward permanent growth
feedback: The shrine's exterior and map state change
The interior presents a contained causal space and a clear completion presentation
consequences: A detour improves both future mobility and long-term capacity
The runtime must preserve many independent challenge, reward, and travel states across the open world
observationThemes.factualDescription: Discovered shrines contain compact challenges, award Spirit Orbs on completion, and function as persistent fast-travel destinations in the wider landscape.
observationThemes.interpretation: A small optional challenge can justify exploration twice when its reward improves both the avatar and the topology of future travel.

observationThemes.context: A dangerous encounter begins while weapons, shields, food, armor, arrows, and escape routes differ in quality or availability.
preconditions: An enemy or boss is present
The player has at least one combat, avoidance, or preparation option
playerInput: Fight, use the environment, eat prepared food, or retreat
Replace gear when durability is exhausted
before: Current health and resources may be insufficient for a direct approach
action: Spend equipment durability and consumables while responding to the encounter
after: The threat is defeated, bypassed, or left for later
Inventory and health reflect the cost
feedback: Damage, weapon-condition warnings, breaks, health, armor, and food effects are visible
Enemy reactions distinguish effective tactics from unsafe ones
consequences: Failure pressure can be answered through cooking, upgrading, route change, stealth, or later return rather than mandatory grinding
observationThemes.factualDescription: Weapons, bows, and shields wear out through use, while food, armor, elemental options, stealth, retreat, and replacement equipment let the player manage encounter risk.
observationThemes.interpretation: Consumable combat power can keep gathering and adaptation relevant when the interface warns clearly and the world supplies multiple recovery strategies.

observationThemes.context: A photograph in the Sheikah Slate depicts an unidentified location associated with Link's missing memories and Zelda's past actions.
preconditions: The corresponding landscape location can be reached
playerInput: Compare the photograph with terrain and NPC information
before: The past event and exact location are unknown
The photograph is only a visual clue
action: Identify the real-world landmark represented by the image
after: The album and narrative understanding gain another piece
feedback: The free camera and landscape allow visual comparison
A cinematic scene clearly separates recovered past from present exploration
consequences: The player can assemble backstory in a self-selected order
Each recovered location ties present exploration to one bounded past event
observationThemes.factualDescription: Stored photographs can be matched to locations in the 3D landscape; reaching the correct site triggers an optional recovered-memory scene about Link, Zelda, and the past.
observationThemes.interpretation: Turning a narrative reveal into visual landmark search lets exploration determine exposition order while keeping the evidence available for deliberate comparison.

contributionHypotheses: Distant landmark visibility can generate self-directed objectives when traversal supports several viable approaches.
Systemic environmental rules are most portable when the same material response informs traversal, puzzle solving, and combat.
Optional challenge nodes can reward curiosity with both avatar growth and a denser future travel network.
Narrative fragments can be discovered nonlinearly when each reveal is anchored to a searchable spatial clue.
uniqueOrNonportable: Hyrule's exact terrain, towers, shrines, Divine Beasts, Sheikah technology, characters, creatures, memory scenes, quest text, music, iconography, and visual language are nonportable.
Specific shrine solutions, Korok tasks, boss weaknesses, recipes, item tables, landmark coordinates, and physics set pieces remain evidence only.
The portable value lies in abstract systemic and spatial relationships, not in reproducing this work's recognizable content density or map silhouette.
openQuestions: A later version delta should measure how the Nintendo Switch 2 Edition's performance, additional save slot, and Zelda Notes change navigation and recovery without treating it as a separate work.
